mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of low-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and retail sales indicates demand overheating that forces monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high-inflation and high-retail periods yield positive 60-day returns in >65% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive-INDPRO-growth months, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts decline exceeding 10% from its 6-month peak signals cooling real estate demand and tightening credit conditions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the housing starts drawdown.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll employment growth and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive-payroll and negative-unemployment periods yield negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In an inflationary expansion regime, persistently low federal funds rates create a policy lag that forces abrupt future tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in >60% of low-rate inflationary periods, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive-GDP-growth quarters, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.
